_meta.keyKnowledge: We reflect on, analyse and document our own and others' drama to inform our creative decisions.

_source.w: We reflect on, analyse and document our own and others' drama to inform our creative decisions.
y: Reflective practice is what separates thoughtful art-making from simply going through the motions.
Analysing drama — our own and others' — deepens our understanding of how theatre communicates.
Documenting our process creates a valuable record of our artistic development.
eg: We maintain a drama journal documenting our creative process, decisions and reflections.
We analyse professional theatre productions and discuss how they achieve their effects.
We use video recordings to evaluate our rehearsals and identify areas for improvement.
We write critical responses to drama we have watched, using appropriate terminology.
We reflect on how feedback from others has influenced our creative decisions.
We compare different interpretations of the same script or stimulus and discuss the choices made.
